'''Thomas Lee''' (born {{birth date|1988|2|1|df=y}}) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Halifax RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Lee has played for eight different clubs during his career.

Lee has previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Salford Red Devils]], [[London Broncos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]] and [[Hull F.C.]]

==Background==
Lee was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England.

==Early career==
As a {{rlp|hb}}, Lee progressed through [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]]'s Academy ranks via amateur club west [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]].<ref>[http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/s/169/169740_hull76_leigh_20.html Hull 76 Leigh 20 – Rugby league – Sport – Manchester Evening News<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

==Senior career==
===Hull F.C. (2005-09)===
Lee made his professional [[rugby league]] début in 2005. Over five-years, Lee made 78 first-grade appearances for [[Hull F.C.]]

===Crusaders RL (2010)===
Lee spent only one season in 2010 at [[Brewery Field]], with the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]].

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats (2011)===
Once again, Lee only spent one season with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the 2011 [[Super League]] season. He went onto record 27 appearances and scored seven tries.

===Huddersfield Giants (2012)===
Lee played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the 2012 [[rugby league]] season. Lee only represented the [[West Yorkshire]] outfit for one season, before moving to the [[London Broncos]].

===London Broncos (2013)===
Lee played one season in the south of [[England]] with the London Broncos in the 2013 campaign.

===Salford Red Devils (2014-16)===
Lee played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the 2014, 2015 and 2016 seasons. Lee made 51 appearances, scoring four tries and kicking two goals, for the team from the [[AJ Bell Stadium]].

===St. Helens (2017)===
The start of the 2017 season marked the fifth time that Lee had represented a club for only a solitary campaign. As he played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] making 19 appearances, before moving back to the city of [[Kingston upon Hull]] to play under Head Coach [[Tim Sheens]], for newly-promoted [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2018 - 2019)===
On 24 October 2017, it was revealed that Lee had signed a two-year contract to play for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2018 and 2019 [[Super League]] seasons.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41748395|title=Tommy Lee and Jordan Walne: Hull KR sign St Helens hooker and Salford prop|publisher=BBC Sport|date=25 October 2017|accessdate=8 December 2017}}</ref>
Lee scored his first try for Hull Kingston Rovers at the 2018 [[Magic Weekend]] against [[Hull F.C.]] On 2019, Lee announced his retirement<ref>{{cite web|website=www.hullkr.co.uk|date=24 Jul 2019|accessdate=7 Jan 2021|title=TOMMY LEE RETIRES FROM RUGBY LEAGUE|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/tommy-lee-retires-from-rugby-league/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 Jul 2019|accessdate=7 Jan 2021|title=Tommy Lee: Hull KR hooker retires after almost 15 years in Super League|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49102354}}</ref>

===Halifax===
On 5 March 2020 it was announced that he had come out of retirement to join [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] to fill the void created by the sudden loss of [[Keal Carlile]], who was forced to retire due to a heart condition<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=6 Mar 2020|accessdate=7 Jan 2021|title=Tommy Lee comes out of retirement to sign for Halifax|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tommy-lee-comes-out-of-retirement-to-sign-for-halifax/}}</ref>
In January 2021, the club web site reported that Lee had been released and would not feature in the 2021 season<ref>{{cite web|website=www.halifaxpanthers.co.uk|date=Jan 2021|accessdate=7 Jan 2021|title=SQUAD UPDATE - LOCKED IN FOR 2021|url=https://halifaxpanthers.co.uk/panthers-news/squad-update-locked-in-for-2021/}}</ref>

==Rugby Union==
On 27 February 2020 it was announced that Lee had signed up to [[Hull RUFC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=www.hullrugbyunion.com|date=27 Feb 2020|accessdate=7 Jan 2021|title=Tommy is getting to grips with union after switching from league|url=https://hullrugbyunion.com/2020/02/27/tommy-is-getting-to-grips-with-union-after-switching-from-league/}}</ref>
